release.deck: Reduces dense Map marker work, speeds Settings, and sheds feed media earlier under WebKit pressure
fixes: Open feeds settings before feed controls assertion
Apply pending friends graph fit all
Show crash recovery when rendering fails

Hidden or locked Freed Desktop sessions now wait longer before logging stale renderer heartbeats, which avoids noisy recovery work during expected WebKit timer throttling while preserving the existing foreground recovery path
Freed Desktop now attributes age-matched WebKit WebContent memory even after the process releases its Freed cache handles, so high WebKit RSS is visible to diagnostics and memory preflight checks
Dense Map fallback rendering now hides lower-priority markers during wheel and pointer movement, reducing paint work while keeping the primary markers reachable
Dense Map views now mount 160 markers instead of 240 and only paint 80 while moving, giving the 1,600 author Map guard more frame headroom on loaded runners
Settings now lazy-mounts section bodies and suppresses expensive blur while actively scrolling or searching
Feed cards now shed inline media when aggregate WebKit RSS reaches 1.5 GB, which matches the pressure range seen in the live dev soak before the old 2 GB threshold fired
followUps: Continue the installed dev build soak and compare WebKit RSS against the v26.5.903 baseline
Continue Map and Friends interaction monitoring under real desktop load
Keeps hidden sessions quieter and restores WebKit memory attribution
Improves dense Friends graph recovery, Map interaction smoothness, desktop crash recovery, and WebKit memory attribution for today's dev builds

releaseBody:
(AI Generated).

## Freed v26.5.906-dev

Reduces dense Map marker work, speeds Settings, and sheds feed media earlier under WebKit pressure

### Fixes

- Open feeds settings before feed controls assertion
- Apply pending friends graph fit all
- Show crash recovery when rendering fails
- Hidden or locked Freed Desktop sessions now wait longer before logging stale renderer heartbeats, which avoids noisy recovery work during expected WebKit timer throttling while preserving the existing foreground recovery path
- Freed Desktop now attributes age-matched WebKit WebContent memory even after the process releases its Freed cache handles, so high WebKit RSS is visible to diagnostics and memory preflight checks
- Dense Map fallback rendering now hides lower-priority markers during wheel and pointer movement, reducing paint work while keeping the primary markers reachable
- Dense Map views now mount 160 markers instead of 240 and only paint 80 while moving, giving the 1,600 author Map guard more frame headroom on loaded runners
- Settings now lazy-mounts section bodies and suppresses expensive blur while actively scrolling or searching
- Feed cards now shed inline media when aggregate WebKit RSS reaches 1.5 GB, which matches the pressure range seen in the live dev soak before the old 2 GB threshold fired

### Follow-ups

- Reduce settings interaction paint work
- Surface metrics to renderer heartbeats
- Stabilize map markers across refreshes
- Relax hidden renderer watchdog
- Continue the installed dev build soak and compare WebKit RSS against the v26.5.903 baseline
- Continue Map and Friends interaction monitoring under real desktop load
- Keeps hidden sessions quieter and restores WebKit memory attribution
- Improves dense Friends graph recovery, Map interaction smoothness, desktop crash recovery, and WebKit memory attribution for today's dev builds

### Downloads

**macOS:** `.dmg` (Apple Silicon or Intel, signed + notarized)  
**Windows:** `.exe` (NSIS installer)  
**Linux:** `.AppImage`  

> macOS downloads are signed and notarized for normal Gatekeeper installation.
